_meta.description: Structured hardware catalog for the Hardware Advisor RAG. Each item has specs + a SEED price. Live prices are refreshed by price_fetch.py; the seed value is the fallback when a live fetch fails.
_meta.currency_note: price_usd is the primary number. price_inr is an approximate convenience value (~85 INR / USD). Always treat seed prices as 'verify live'.

gpus.name: NVIDIA RTX 3060 12GB
gpus.good_for: Cheapest sane entry into local AI. Runs 7B LLMs in 4-bit, Stable Diffusion, learning. CUDA works everywhere.
gpus.watch_out: 12GB caps you at ~7B models and small fine-tunes. No headroom for 13B+.

gpus.name: NVIDIA RTX 4060 Ti 16GB
gpus.good_for: Best value NEW card for small local AI in 2026. 16GB runs 7B at FP16 / 13B in 4-bit, low power.
gpus.watch_out: Narrow 128-bit memory bus -> slower than its number suggests for big batches.

gpus.name: NVIDIA RTX 3090 24GB (used)
gpus.good_for: The value king of local AI in 2026: 24GB VRAM for ~$700 used. Runs 13B at FP16, 34B in 4-bit, QLoRA fine-tuning.
gpus.watch_out: Used -> check warranty/condition. High 350W power draw, needs a strong PSU (750W+).

gpus.name: NVIDIA RTX 4090 24GB
gpus.good_for: Fastest 24GB consumer card. Same VRAM as 3090 but much faster compute -> serious fine-tuning + fast inference.
gpus.watch_out: Expensive and power-hungry (450W). If you only need the VRAM, a used 3090 is far cheaper.

gpus.name: NVIDIA RTX 5090 32GB
gpus.good_for: Best consumer GPU for AI in 2026. 32GB GDDR7 runs 7B at full FP16, QLoRA on 13B-34B, all Stable Diffusion/ComfyUI.
gpus.watch_out: 575W TDP needs an 1000W+ PSU and good cooling. Premium price.

gpus.name: NVIDIA H100 80GB (enterprise)
gpus.good_for: Production-standard data-center GPU for large-scale training/inference. 80GB HBM3.
gpus.watch_out: Buying makes sense only at scale. For most projects, RENT it on the cloud instead (see cloud options).

gpus.name: NVIDIA B200 (Blackwell, enterprise)
gpus.good_for: Newest Blackwell data-center GPU, tuned for LLM training + long-context. Best raw performance available.
gpus.watch_out: Enterprise-only, extreme cost + power. Rent via cloud unless you run a data center.

cpus.name: AMD Ryzen 5 7600
cpus.good_for: Plenty for feeding a single GPU in an AI/dev box, and for general use + gaming. Low power.
cpus.watch_out: 6 cores is light for heavy CPU data-preprocessing or multi-GPU rigs.

cpus.name: AMD Ryzen 7 7700X
cpus.good_for: Sweet spot for a serious AI workstation: enough cores for data prep + dataloading without bottlenecking the GPU.
cpus.watch_out: Overkill for a pure inference box where the GPU does everything.

cpus.name: AMD Ryzen 9 7950X
cpus.good_for: Heavy CPU preprocessing, multi-GPU feeding, video work. 16 cores chew through dataloaders.
cpus.watch_out: Expensive + hot. Single-GPU inference rigs won't use all these cores.

ram.name: 32GB DDR5-6000 (2x16GB)
ram.good_for: Baseline for an AI workstation. Rule of thumb: system RAM >= 2x your GPU VRAM.
ram.watch_out: If you load big datasets in RAM or run CPU-offload for huge models, jump to 64GB+.

ram.name: 64GB DDR5-6000 (2x32GB)
ram.good_for: For 24GB+ GPUs, big datasets, or running large models partly on CPU (offload). Comfortable headroom.
ram.watch_out: Diminishing returns for pure inference unless you offload to CPU.

psu.name: 750W 80+ Gold PSU
psu.good_for: Enough for a single 3090/4080-class GPU (<=350W) + a mid CPU. Headroom rule: PSU >= 1.5x total component draw.
psu.watch_out: Not enough for a 4090 (450W) or 5090 (575W) build.

psu.name: 1000W 80+ Gold PSU
psu.good_for: Required for RTX 4090 (450W) or 5090 (575W) builds with headroom. Safe for one big GPU + strong CPU.
psu.watch_out: Overkill (wasted money) for an entry/budget single-GPU build.

cloud_gpus.name: RTX 4090 24GB (cloud, RunPod-class)
cloud_gpus.good_for: Rent a 4090 by the hour. Great when you need power occasionally and don't want to buy. ~$0.69/hr.
cloud_gpus.watch_out: If you run >~8 hrs/day every day, buying may beat renting within months. Do the math (see local_vs_cloud).

cloud_gpus.name: A100 80GB (cloud)
cloud_gpus.good_for: 80GB for big fine-tunes / mid training without owning data-center hardware. ~$1.6/hr.
cloud_gpus.watch_out: Costs add up fast for long runs. Use spot/community pricing and checkpoint often.

cloud_gpus.name: H100 80GB (cloud)
cloud_gpus.good_for: Production-grade training power on demand. ~$2.99/hr. The sane way to touch H100s for most teams.
cloud_gpus.watch_out: Expensive for idle time. Only spin up for active training; tear down immediately after.
